ULTRA_CRITICAL_FOR_GPT_MODELS_READ_FIRST.MODEL_NOTE: These instructions are optimized for GPT-5-mini to avoid common scoring mistakes
MOST_IMPORTANT_RULES: If you see 'stay on the line' or 'please hold' and NO LIVE HUMAN PROSPECT speaks after that = Score 0 (NOT 5)
If transcript ends abruptly during agent's incomplete sentence = Score 1 (mid-sentence hangup)
If prospect says 'I'll transfer you' but NO ONE picks up = Score 4 (failed transfer)
'Tomorrow afternoon' without specific time + no email = Score 5 (NOT 9)
Fake/profane email or nonsensical responses = Score 1 (time waster)
Name only without contact info = Score 5 (NOT 6)
ULTRA_CRITICAL_FOR_GPT_MODELS_READ_FIRST.SCORE_INFLATION_WARNING: GPT models tend to inflate scores. When in doubt, choose LOWER score. Be conservative.

task: Read the entire call transcript and assign one integer engagement score from 0 to 10 that reflects the prospect's level of interest and participation during this call only.

CRITICAL_RULE_CHECK_FIRST.instruction: BEFORE ANYTHING ELSE: Use this TWO-STEP process to check for Score 0 scenarios

STEP_1_DETECT_AI_RECEPTIONIST_FIRST.question: Is this an AI receptionist/virtual assistant call?
detection_phrases: Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with
It's a great day at [company name]
This is [name]. Who do I have the pleasure

OVERLY_SCRIPTED_LANGUAGE_INDICATORS.pattern: Repetitive formal phrases that sound automated/scripted
examples: Repeating 'Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with' multiple times
'It's a great day at [Company]' opening
Overly formal language combined with inability to provide contact details
Says 'I don't have specific contact details' + 'I'll make a note' pattern
OVERLY_SCRIPTED_LANGUAGE_INDICATORS.action: If detected + NO specific email provided + NO decision-maker contact given = Score 0

STEP_2_CHECK_FOR_EMAIL_EXCEPTION.rule: If AI receptionist detected, CHECK FOR SPECIFIC EMAIL before returning Score 0

examples: dominic@mainely-electrical.com = Score 5
john@acmeelectric.com = Score 5
sarah@smithco.com = Score 5
info@company.com = Score 0
contact@company.com = Score 0

no_email_only_name.pattern: AI provides name like 'Stainless Lombard' but NO email/phone
examples: Talk to John (no contact) = Score 0
Stainless Lombard handles payroll (no email) = Score 0

no_email_no_name.pattern: AI takes message but provides NO decision-maker name AND NO email/phone
examples: 'I'll pass along your message' (no name, no contact) = Score 0
'Someone will get back to you' (no specific person or contact) = Score 0
'I don't have access to staff details' (no actionable info) = Score 0

STEP_3_CHECK_FOR_LIVE_PROSPECT_PICKUP.CRITICAL_INSTRUCTION: After 'stay on the line' or 'please hold', carefully check if a NEW LIVE PROSPECT (different from gatekeeper) actually speaks
verification_steps: Step 1: Identify the gatekeeper/screener (person who said 'stay on the line')
Step 2: Look for a DIFFERENT person introducing themselves AFTER the hold
Step 3: Check if this new person engages with the agent
Step 4: If YES (new person picked up) → Score their engagement (NOT 0)
Step 5: If NO (silence, or call ends) → Score 0
examples_NOT_zero: Gatekeeper: 'Stay on the line' → USER: 'Hi, this is Deb from King of Hawes' → Score Deb's engagement (NOT 0)
Screener: 'Please hold' → USER: 'This is John, how can I help?' → Score John's engagement (NOT 0)
Receptionist: 'I'll see if available' → USER: 'Hey, this is Sarah' → Score Sarah's engagement (NOT 0)
examples_ARE_zero: Gatekeeper: 'Stay on the line' → [silence] → [call ends] = Score 0
Receptionist: 'I'll see if available' → [no response] = Score 0

RETURN_ZERO_IMMEDIATELY_IF_YOU_SEE: ANY voicemail greeting or 'leave a message' (unless SPECIFIC email provided by AI receptionist)
'Stay on the line' with NO DIFFERENT PERSON pickup after (silence or call ends)
'Please hold' with NO NEW PERSON speaking after (silence or call ends)
'I'll see if this person is available' + NO NEW PERSON speaks (silence or call ends)
'Record your name and reason' + NO NEW PERSON speaks after recording
AI receptionist provides ONLY name (no email/phone) + no live human = 0
AI receptionist provides GENERIC email (info@, contact@) + no live human = 0
Gatekeeper screening + NO actual decision-maker speaks (no new person picks up)
Call ends during hold/transfer with NO new person engagement
AI receptionist/voicemail takes message but provides NO name AND NO contact info = 0

CRITICAL_EXCEPTION_DO_NOT_RETURN_ZERO_IF.live_prospect_picked_up: If 'stay on the line' BUT a NEW LIVE PERSON (different from gatekeeper) picks up and speaks → Score THEIR engagement (NOT 0)
CRITICAL_EXCEPTION_DO_NOT_RETURN_ZERO_IF.ai_receptionist_specific_email: AI receptionist provides SPECIFIC personal email (name@company.com) = Score 5, NOT 0
verification_checklist: Step 2: Did they provide an email?
Step 3: Is it SPECIFIC (dominic@company.com) or GENERIC (info@company.com)?
Step 4: If SPECIFIC → Score 5 | If GENERIC or NO EMAIL → Score 0
verification_checklist_screening: Step 1: Detect 'stay on the line' or 'please hold'
Step 2: Did a DIFFERENT person (not gatekeeper) introduce themselves after?
Step 3: If YES → Score their engagement | If NO → Score 0
examples_score_5_not_0: AI: 'Reach out to john@smithco.com' = Score 5 (specific email exception)
examples_score_their_engagement_not_0: Gatekeeper: 'Stay on the line' → Deb: 'Hi, this is Deb. We're busy, call next week' = Score 3 (Deb engaged, resistant)
Screener: 'Please hold' → John: 'This is John, not interested' = Score 2 (John engaged, dismissive)
examples_score_0: AI: 'Stainless Lombard handles payroll' (name only) = Score 0

look_for_these_exact_phrases: I'll see if this person is available
HOW_TO_DETECT_NO_LIVE_PROSPECT.then_check: Did a NEW LIVE PROSPECT (different person than gatekeeper) speak AFTER these phrases? If NO → Check email exception first, then Score 0. If YES → Score the new person's engagement.

VOICEMAIL_SCREENING_EXAMPLES.example_1: USER: 'I'll see if available' → AGENT: [explains] → USER: 'Stay on the line' → [no pickup] = Score 0
VOICEMAIL_SCREENING_EXAMPLES.example_2: USER: 'Record your name' → AGENT: [records] → [no prospect speaks] = Score 0
VOICEMAIL_SCREENING_EXAMPLES.example_4_AI_no_email: AI: 'Stainless Lombard handles payroll' (name only, no email) = Score 0
VOICEMAIL_SCREENING_EXAMPLES.example_6_pickup_happened: Gatekeeper: 'Stay on the line' → Deb: 'Hi, this is Deb, we're busy' = Score 3 (NOT 0, someone picked up)
VOICEMAIL_SCREENING_EXAMPLES.example_7_AI_no_info: AI: 'I'll pass your message along' (no name, no email given) = Score 0

CRITICAL_RULE_CHECK_FIRST.DEFAULT_BEHAVIOR: When in doubt whether NEW live prospect spoke after screening, assume NO. But ALWAYS check if a DIFFERENT PERSON picked up after 'stay on line' - if they did, score their engagement. ALWAYS check for specific email exception before returning 0 for AI receptionist calls.

OVERRIDE_ALL_OTHER_SCORES.rule: Score 0 takes ABSOLUTE PRIORITY over all other scores, EXCEPT when live prospect picked up OR specific email exception applies
OVERRIDE_ALL_OTHER_SCORES.instruction: If ANY Score 0 condition is met AND no new person picked up, immediately return 0 UNLESS AI receptionist provided specific personal email (then Score 5)
OVERRIDE_ALL_OTHER_SCORES.hierarchy: Live prospect pickup (score their engagement) > Specific email exception (Score 5) > Score 0 > all other scores
examples: AI receptionist provides name 'John Smith' only → Score 0 (no live human, no email)
AI receptionist provides 'john@company.com' → Score 5 (email exception overrides Score 0)
Voicemail gives generic 'info@company.com' → Score 0 (generic email doesn't count)
Gatekeeper transfers but no one picks up → Score 0 (no live prospect spoke)
Gatekeeper: 'Stay on line' → Deb picks up and says 'busy, call next week' → Score 3 (Deb engaged, NOT 0)

scope.call_only: Score this call only. Ignore historical context.
scope.focus_subject: Evaluate only the prospect's words, tone, and behavior.

output_requirements.strict_format: Return only the integer (e.g., 8). No labels, JSON, prose, or extra characters.

0.label: Non-Evaluable or No Live Human
0.CRITICAL_EMPHASIS: Score 0 is for ANY call where NO live human prospect engaged beyond automated responses or greetings
rules: Voicemail box reached (any automated greeting without direct contact info)
IVR/phone tree only (press 1, press 2, automated menu)
Hold music with no prospect pickup
AI receptionist with NO live human interaction
Voicemail screening: 'I'll see if available' + 'stay on line' + NO pickup
'Record your name' + agent records + NO live prospect speaks
'Please hold' or 'stay on the line' + call ends or silence
Gatekeeper screens but NO decision-maker ever speaks
Wrong number, accidental pickup, immediate disconnect
Call duration < 15 seconds with no substance

SPECIFIC_PATTERNS_THAT_ARE_ZERO.pattern_1: USER: 'I'll see if [person] is available' → 'Stay on the line' → No one picks up = 0
SPECIFIC_PATTERNS_THAT_ARE_ZERO.pattern_2: USER: 'Please hold' → Hold music or silence → No prospect = 0
SPECIFIC_PATTERNS_THAT_ARE_ZERO.pattern_3: USER: 'Record your name' → Agent records → No prospect speaks = 0
SPECIFIC_PATTERNS_THAT_ARE_ZERO.pattern_4: AI: 'I'm an AI receptionist' → Takes message → No human = 0

0.NOT_SCORE_0: If after 'stay on the line', a LIVE PERSON (not gatekeeper) actually speaks and engages, then score their behavior (not 0)

NOT_SCORE_0_IF_CONVERSATION_THEN_VOICEMA.rule: If live person had a SUBSTANTIVE EXCHANGE (not just screening) then transferred to voicemail = Score 3 (not 0)
substantive_exchange_means: Explained they're not the right person
examples_score_3: USER: 'I'm not in charge of that. I can transfer you to their voicemail' = 3 (had conversation first)
AGENT: 'Who handles payroll?' USER: 'That's Mary, let me send you to her voicemail' = 3 (exchange happened)
STILL_SCORE_0_screening_only: USER: 'Stay on the line' → voicemail = 0 (pure screening, no exchange)
USER: 'I'll see if available' → voicemail = 0 (screening, no conversation)
NOT_SCORE_0_IF_CONVERSATION_THEN_VOICEMA.key_test: Was there actual dialogue/exchange before voicemail? YES = 3, NO = 0

1.label: Hostile or Angry
1.CRITICAL_EMPHASIS: Includes mid-sentence hangups, fake emails, and intentional time wasting

MID_SENTENCE_HANGUP_DETECTION.rule: Hangup is Score 1 ONLY if it appears HOSTILE or INTENTIONAL interruption
MID_SENTENCE_HANGUP_DETECTION.how_to_detect: Look for agent's sentence that ends with '...' or incomplete thought AND hostile context
hostile_context_indicators: Prospect was dismissive or irritated before hangup
Prospect said 'don't call' or 'not interested' right before
Hangup happens during prospect's angry statement
NOT_hostile_hangup: Prospect said 'call back later' or 'busy' before disconnect
Prospect was polite but needed to go
Call just ends awkwardly but no hostility shown
Prospect said 'call tomorrow' or 'I'm working' before disconnect = Score 3 (timing issue, not hostile)
examples_NOT_score_1: USER: 'Call tomorrow, I'm busy' AGENT: 'I completely...' [ends] = 3 (polite exit, timing issue)
USER: 'Can't talk now' AGENT: 'Just one...' [ends] = 3 (resistant but not hostile)

rules: Profanity, insults, threats, mocking, yelling
Aggressive 'never call again' with hostile tone
Hangs up WHILE agent is speaking WITH HOSTILE CONTEXT (dismissive, angry, rude)
Transcript ends abruptly during agent's incomplete sentence WITH HOSTILE INTENT
Hangs up during hostile statement
Intentionally wasting time (changes topic 2+ times, nonsensical responses)
Fake/profane email addresses (contains insults, profanity, mocking words)
Gibberish responses throughout call ('I'm a potato', 'Yes. Seven one. Eighty three.')
Obviously trolling or mocking the agent

SCRIPTED_TIME_WASTING_PATTERN.rule: Overly formal/scripted responses + collects agent info + provides ZERO actionable information = Score 1 (time wasting, NOT Score 3)
detection_pattern: Uses repetitive formal phrases ('Who do I have the pleasure', 'It's a great day at [Company]')
Says 'I don't have specific contact details' or 'I don't have access to'
Promises 'I'll make a note' or 'someone will call you back'
Provides NO name, NO email, NO direct contact
SCRIPTED_TIME_WASTING_PATTERN.rationale: This behavior intentionally wastes agent's time by creating illusion of helpfulness while delivering zero actionable information
SCRIPTED_TIME_WASTING_PATTERN.priority: This rule OVERRIDES Score 3 gatekeeper rules when overly scripted language is detected
examples_score_1: 'It's a great day at [Company]' + 'Who do I have the pleasure' (repeated) + takes agent info + 'I don't have specific contact details' = 1
Overly formal receptionist takes full agent details + 'I'll make a note' + gives nothing = 1

patterns: Obviously fake emails (screw.you@, fake@, troll@, etc.)
Unrealistic data combined with fake email (3200 employees + fake email = troll)

ABUSE_WITH_INFO_RULE.rule: If prospect uses abusive or vulgar language during the call, apply this two-step test

STEP_1_CHECK_INFO_QUALITY.question: Did the prospect provide real, actionable information despite the abuse?

STEP_2_APPLY_SCORE.if_real_info_provided: IGNORE the abuse completely. Score normally per all other rules.

examples: Prospect says 'Your penis' but gives real EHR + real email + books demo = Score normally (ignore abuse)
Prospect curses once but confirms company + role + specific time = Score normally (ignore abuse)
Prospect abuses throughout + gives fake email + nonsensical answers = Score 1
Prospect abuses + gives NO useful info at all = Score 1
ABUSE_WITH_INFO_RULE.key_test: Is the info real and actionable? YES = ignore abuse, score normally. NO = Score 1.

2.label: Actively Dismissive
2.CRITICAL_NOTE: Rejection of the OFFER, not timing. 'Not interested' ≠ 'Can't talk'
rules: Clear rejection: 'not interested,' 'we're all set,' 'we're good'
Calm 'don't call again' / 'remove me' / DNC request
'Email me' used purely to exit with zero info
Says rejection THEN hangs up (completes sentence)
'We use [system]' mentioned ONLY to justify rejection (e.g., 'We use Square, we're all set')

CURRENT_STATE_IN_REJECTION_CONTEXT.rule: If prospect mentions current state BUT uses it to dismiss you, score as 2 (not 7)
CURRENT_STATE_IN_REJECTION_CONTEXT.example: 'We're all set. We use Square.' = Score 2 (dismissive, not interested)
CURRENT_STATE_IN_REJECTION_CONTEXT.contrast: 'We use Square. How much is yours?' = Score 7 (interested)

3.label: Resistant
3.CRITICAL_NOTE: 'Can't talk' is Score 3 (timing issue), NOT Score 2 (rejection)

GATEKEEPER_COLLECTS_INFO_GIVES_NOTHING.rule: If gatekeeper takes ALL agent's information BUT provides ZERO prospect information = Score 3 (NOT 5)
GATEKEEPER_COLLECTS_INFO_GIVES_NOTHING.EXCEPTION: If gatekeeper uses OVERLY SCRIPTED language (see Score 1 SCRIPTED_TIME_WASTING_PATTERN) = Score 1 (NOT 3)
GATEKEEPER_COLLECTS_INFO_GIVES_NOTHING.pattern: Gatekeeper asks for agent's name/phone/details + says 'I'll have someone contact you' or 'I'll pass this along' + gives NO name, NO email, NO direct contact
GATEKEEPER_COLLECTS_INFO_GIVES_NOTHING.key_test: Did gatekeeper provide ANY actionable prospect information? If NO = Score 3
examples_score_3: Gatekeeper: 'I'll have our team contact you' (takes agent info, gives nothing) = 3
Gatekeeper: 'I'll have a specialist reach out' (collects agent details, provides zero prospect info) = 3
Gatekeeper: 'I'll make sure our team gets your message' (takes all info, gives no name/contact) = 3
Gatekeeper: 'Someone will get back to you' (collected agent info, provided nothing) = 3
GATEKEEPER_COLLECTS_INFO_GIVES_NOTHING.contrast_with_score_5: Score 5 requires actual name OR contact provided. Pure deflection with zero prospect info = Score 3

AWKWARD_CALL_ENDINGS.rule: If prospect said 'call back' or 'busy' and call ends awkwardly = Score 3 (not 1)
AWKWARD_CALL_ENDINGS.rationale: Polite timing issue, not hostile interruption
examples: 'Call tomorrow, I'm working' → call ends during agent response = 3
'Too busy right now' → call disconnects = 3
'Can't talk now' → abrupt end = 3
'Call me tomorrow please, I'm working on project' → ends mid-agent-sentence = 3 (polite context)

NOT_DECISION_MAKER_NO_REFERRAL.rule: If prospect says 'I'm not the decision maker' AND provides NO decision-maker name or contact = Score 3
patterns: 'I'm not the one who makes decisions' + no referral given = 3
'I would have to find out and get back to you' (gives nothing) = 3
'I need to check who handles that' (provides no name/contact) = 3
Explains they're not decision-maker + takes agent info + gives ZERO prospect info = 3
NOT_DECISION_MAKER_NO_REFERRAL.key_test: Did they actually PROVIDE a name or contact? If NO = Score 3. If YES (even just name) = Score 5.
examples: 'I'm not the decision maker. I'll find out and get back to you' = 3 (nothing provided)
'Not me, talk to John' (gives name) = 5 (name provided)
'I'm not in charge. Email sarah@company.com' = 6 (name + contact)

rules: Polite but closed: 'can't talk,' 'busy,' 'thanks, bye'
Very short responses (< 20 words total)
Call ends awkwardly after 'call back later' or 'busy' = 3 (not 1)
'Not the right person' + ends call without referral
'I'm not the decision maker' + provides NO name or contact info = 3
Name provided BUT contact is 'same number' / 'we route calls' / no direct line = 3 (NOT 5)
'I'll find out and get back to you' but gives NOTHING = 3
Gatekeeper blocks and shows NO interest in helping
Gatekeeper says 'I don't have a direct line I could give you'
Takes agent's info but provides ZERO prospect information
'Can't talk right now' + immediate exit (timing issue, not rejection)
'Call back tomorrow' or 'Call me tomorrow' used as POLITE EXIT with NO engagement (no questions, no info, just trying to end call)
Busy + suggests callback but showed ZERO engagement or interest signals
Spoke with live person who had exchange (2+ turns) then transferred to voicemail
Name provided BUT contact is 'same number' / 'we route calls' / no direct line = 3 (NOT 5)

POLITE_EXIT_CALLBACK.rule: If prospect is busy and says 'call tomorrow' BUT showed NO engagement = Score 3 (not 5)
POLITE_EXIT_CALLBACK.pattern: Prospect says they're busy/can't talk + suggests callback to exit politely
POLITE_EXIT_CALLBACK.key_test: Did they engage AT ALL? (ask question, provide info, show interest?)
examples_score_3: 'I'm busy right now. Call me tomorrow?' = 3 (just trying to exit)
'Under the gun. Call back tomorrow?' = 3 (no engagement)
examples_score_5: 'Sounds interesting. Can you call tomorrow?' = 5 (showed interest)
'Let me think about it. Call tomorrow' = 5 (acknowledged offer)

NAME_WITH_GENERIC_OFFICE_NUMBER.rule: If name provided BUT contact is generic office number/main line = Score 3 (NOT 5)
NAME_WITH_GENERIC_OFFICE_NUMBER.pattern: Gatekeeper provides decision-maker name + 'office number' or 'main line' or 'same number you called'
NAME_WITH_GENERIC_OFFICE_NUMBER.rationale: Generic routing number means another screening call - no direct access gained
examples_score_3: 'Talk to Amy. Call the office number' = 3 (will be screened again)
'Contact Sarah at this number' (main line) = 3 (no direct line)
examples_score_5: 'Talk to Amy' (name only, no contact mentioned) = 5
NAME_WITH_GENERIC_OFFICE_NUMBER.key_distinction: Score 5 = name without contact. Score 3 = name with generic/routing number that requires re-screening.

EMPLOYEE_NOT_DECISION_MAKER_TO_VOICEMAIL.rule: Score 3 if person explicitly states they are NOT the decision-maker/not in charge, THEN sends to voicemail
EMPLOYEE_NOT_DECISION_MAKER_TO_VOICEMAIL.required_pattern: Person must say 'I'm not in charge' or 'I don't handle that' or similar self-identification
examples_score_3_only: 'I'm not in charge of that. I can transfer to voicemail' = 3
'I don't handle payroll. Let me send you to voicemail' = 3

CANT_TALK_VS_NOT_INTERESTED.score_3: 'Can't talk right now' / 'Busy' / 'Bad timing' = Score 3 (resistant, timing issue)
CANT_TALK_VS_NOT_INTERESTED.score_2: 'Not interested' / 'We're all set' / 'We're good' = Score 2 (dismissive, active rejection)
CANT_TALK_VS_NOT_INTERESTED.key_difference: Score 3 = timing issue. Score 2 = don't want the product/service.

phrases: 'I would not be the right person'
'I can take your message' (but gives nothing)
GATEKEEPER_BLOCKING_PATTERN.action: Takes AGENT'S name/number but gives NO decision-maker name or contact

4.label: Reluctant / Polite Exit
4.CRITICAL_NOTE: Includes failed transfers WHERE NO NAME WAS GIVEN
rules: Generic deflection: 'send me an email,' 'call back later'
Vague callback: 'call back sometime' (no timeframe)
'Talk to HR' without name or contact
Gatekeeper mentions name but call doesn't connect
Promised transfer but FAILED and NO decision-maker name given
Generic email address (info@, contact@, admin@) without decision-maker contact

FAILED_TRANSFER_WITHOUT_NAME.rule: If transfer promised but FAILED and NO decision-maker name given = Score 4
FAILED_TRANSFER_WITHOUT_NAME.pattern: USER: 'Let me transfer you' → Hold → 'Are you still there?' → No response
FAILED_TRANSFER_WITHOUT_NAME.reason: Pure deflection - promised help but provided nothing
FAILED_TRANSFER_WITHOUT_NAME.key_check: Did we get a NAME? If NO = Score 4. If YES = Score 5.

5.label: Neutral / Functional
5.CRITICAL_NOTE: Name OR contact (not both). Vague callbacks. MUST ACTUALLY PROVIDE INFORMATION (not promise to find out).

REQUIRES_ACTUAL_INFO.rule: Score 5 requires ACTUAL name or contact provided, not just a promise
score_5_NO_these_are_3: 'I'll find out who handles that' (nothing given) = 3
'I would have to get back to you' (nothing given) = 3
REQUIRES_ACTUAL_INFO.key_distinction: Actual info provided = 5. Promise to find out later = 3.

EXCEPTION_AI_RECEPTIONIST.rule: If an AI receptionist/virtual assistant provides a name but NO contact info AND no live human speaks = Score 0 (not 5)
EXCEPTION_AI_RECEPTIONIST.rule_exception: If AI receptionist provides SPECIFIC, DIRECT email (name@company.com) = Score 5 even without live human
EXCEPTION_AI_RECEPTIONIST.rationale: AI-provided names alone without human engagement are non-evaluable, BUT specific email is actionable
EXCEPTION_AI_RECEPTIONIST.detection: Look for 'AI receptionist', 'virtual assistant', 'automated assistant' in opening
EXCEPTION_AI_RECEPTIONIST.action_if_name_only: AI + name only + no live human = Score 0
EXCEPTION_AI_RECEPTIONIST.action_if_email_provided: AI + specific email + no live human = Score 5 (email exception applies)

rules: Answers questions when asked but shows no initiative
No meaningful questions from prospect
Provides name OR contact method (not both) - MUST BE FROM LIVE HUMAN (includes name + 'same number' or 'call this number')
Name provided with phone number instruction (even if same number as called) = 5
Vague callback: 'tomorrow afternoon' without SPECIFIC TIME
Voicemail WITH direct email or phone number
Receptionist gives decision-maker name WITHOUT contact info
'Ask for [Name]' without providing phone/email
Failed transfer but got decision-maker NAME (even if no contact) = 5

NAME_WITH_PHONE_INSTRUCTION.rule: If gatekeeper provides decision-maker name + phone instruction (even 'same number') = Score 5
NAME_WITH_PHONE_INSTRUCTION.rationale: We captured name and have a number to reach them, even if requires re-screening

NEAR_TERM_CALLBACK_REQUIRES_ENGAGEMENT.rule: Score 5 callbacks require prospect showed SOME engagement beyond just being busy

must_have_at_least_one: Answered at least ONE question about their business/current state
Asked at least ONE question about the offer
Acknowledged what the offer is specifically (not just 'I can go over it')
NOT_engagement: 'I can go over it tomorrow' = NOT engagement (just setting time)
'Call me back and we can discuss' = NOT engagement (generic)
'I'm busy, call tomorrow' = NOT engagement (polite exit)

examples_score_5: Prospect answered 'What are you using?' THEN said 'call tomorrow' = 5
Prospect asked 'What do you offer?' THEN requested callback = 5
examples_score_3: Prospect just said 'I'm busy, call tomorrow' with ZERO engagement = 3
Immediate exit with callback suggestion, no questions answered = 3
'Call me back tomorrow and I can go over it' but answered nothing = 3

ANSWERING_QUESTIONS_ONLY.rule: If prospect ANSWERS agent's questions about current state (doesn't volunteer it) = Score 5
ANSWERING_QUESTIONS_ONLY.REQUIRES_MINIMAL_ENGAGEMENT: Score 5 requires prospect answered AT LEAST ONE question or provided some info. Just saying 'call tomorrow' with ZERO engagement = Score 3
ANSWERING_QUESTIONS_ONLY.pattern: Agent asks → User provides answer → No proactive disclosure
examples_score_5: Agent: 'What are you using?' User: 'QuickBooks' (answered when asked) = 5
Agent: 'Do you have a CPA?' User: 'No' (just responding) = 5
examples_score_3_NOT_5: 'I'm busy, call tomorrow' (NO questions answered, NO info given) = 3
'Can't talk now, try later' (pure polite exit) = 3
ANSWERING_QUESTIONS_ONLY.key_distinction: Answering questions = Score 5. Just callback request with zero engagement = Score 3.

FAILED_TRANSFER_WITH_NAME.rule: If transfer fails BUT we captured decision-maker NAME = Score 5 (not 4)
FAILED_TRANSFER_WITH_NAME.rationale: We got actionable info (the name), even though transfer failed
FAILED_TRANSFER_WITH_NAME.example: USER: 'That's Jesse, let me transfer.' [holds] [fails] = Score 5 (we know Jesse's name)
FAILED_TRANSFER_WITH_NAME.contrast: USER: 'Let me transfer you to HR.' [holds] [fails, never gave name] = Score 4 (pure deflection)
FAILED_TRANSFER_WITH_NAME.key_distinction: Score 5 = NAME captured. Score 4 = NO name captured.

VAGUE_TIME_EXAMPLES.NOT_score_5: 'Tomorrow at 2 PM' = specific time (could be 9 if email captured)

examples: 'Talk to Jesse' (no phone/email) = 5
'Ask for Mary' (no direct contact) = 5
Transfer fails but got name 'John' = 5

6.label: Mild Interest
6.CRITICAL_NOTE: Requires name AND contact (both), OR one single question
rules: One light/high-level question with no follow-up or disclosure
Tentative openness: 'maybe later,' 'could be relevant'
Name AND direct contact provided (email/phone/extension) from LIVE person
'Send pricing to [specific email]' (contact captured)
Successful transfer to decision-maker who engages
Single question but no current state disclosed

NAME_AND_CONTACT_REQUIREMENT.must_have_both: Name AND (email OR phone OR extension)

6.SINGLE_QUESTION_RULE: One question alone with no follow-up = 6 (not 7)

7.label: Interested
7.CRITICAL_NOTE: Requires meaningful exploration, not just one question. Current state must be VOLUNTEERED, not just answered.
rules: VOLUNTEERS current state (NOT just answering when asked): 'We use ADP,' 'Doing it manually,' 'CPA handles it'
Asks substantive question + provides additional context
Mentions competitor by name in exploratory way
Describes pain point or requirement
Multiple questions OR one question + VOLUNTEERED current state disclosure

CRITICAL_DISTINCTION_VOLUNTEERED_VS_ANSW.score_7_REQUIRES: Prospect brings up current state WITHOUT being asked
CRITICAL_DISTINCTION_VOLUNTEERED_VS_ANSW.score_5_when: Agent asks 'What are you using?' → Prospect answers 'QuickBooks' = Score 5 (NOT 7)
examples_score_7: 'We use QuickBooks. How much is yours?' (volunteered) = 7
'I'm doing it manually, what would this cost?' (volunteered) = 7
examples_score_5_NOT_7: Agent: 'What are you using?' User: 'QuickBooks' (just answering) = 5
Agent: 'Do you have a CPA?' User: 'No' (just answering) = 5

CURRENT_STATE_MUST_BE_EXPLORATORY.score_7: 'We use QuickBooks. How much is yours?' = interested/exploring
CURRENT_STATE_MUST_BE_EXPLORATORY.score_2: 'We use Square, we're all set' = dismissive rejection

CURRENT_STATE_MUST_BE_VOLUNTEERED.rule: Score 7 requires prospect VOLUNTEERS current state or asks substantive question WITH additional context
score_7_YES: 'We use QuickBooks. How much is yours?' (volunteered + question) = 7
'I'm doing it manually, what would this cost?' (volunteered + question) = 7
score_7_NO_these_are_5: 'What do you offer?' (generic question, no volunteered info) = 5
CURRENT_STATE_MUST_BE_VOLUNTEERED.key_distinction: Volunteered disclosure + substantive engagement = 7. Just answering questions = 5.

8.label: Engaged
8.CRITICAL_NOTE: 'Tell me more' or 'I'm interested' with questions = clear engagement signal
rules: Multiple meaningful questions or deep-dive conversation
Volunteers context: current tools, pain points, constraints, volume
Soft next-step intent: 'Maybe next week,' 'I'd like a demo,' 'What's the next step?'
'Tell me more' + asks follow-up questions
'I'm interested, tell me more' = sustained engagement
Shows sustained interest beyond initial curiosity
Three or more questions in total
Asks about multiple aspects (cost, integration, features)

TELL_ME_MORE_SIGNAL.rule: If prospect says 'tell me more' after asking questions = Score 8 (not 7)
TELL_ME_MORE_SIGNAL.pattern: Multiple questions + 'tell me more' or 'interesting, tell me more' = clear engagement
TELL_ME_MORE_SIGNAL.example: USER: 'We use ADP. How much? Does it integrate? What about taxes?' + 'Tell me more' = Score 8

9.label: Highly Engaged
9.CRITICAL_NOTE: Requires SPECIFIC time AND contact captured. Missing any field = NOT 10.
rules: Accepts or proposes SPECIFIC time (e.g., 'Friday at 2 PM', 'Tomorrow at 10 AM')
Provides contact info: email address OR phone number captured
Asks detailed evaluation questions (pricing details, implementation, timeline)
Requests materials for internal review
Concrete next step BUT missing one or more of the 5 qualification fields

SPECIFIC_TIME_REQUIREMENT.score_9_requires: SPECIFIC time like 'Friday at 2 PM' or 'Tomorrow at 10 AM'

MISSING_FIELDS_CHECK.if_missing_any: If missing company, role, or current state = Score 9 (not 10)
MISSING_FIELDS_CHECK.all_5_required_for_10: Time, Email, Company, Role, Current State

10.label: Champion / Committed
10.ABSOLUTE_REQUIREMENT: ALL 5 FIELDS BELOW MUST BE EXPLICITLY PRESENT. IF EVEN ONE IS MISSING, SCORE IS 9.

field_2_email.reject_fake: Fake/profane emails do NOT count (score as 1 instead)

VERIFICATION_CHECKLIST: Step 1: Count how many of the 5 fields are EXPLICITLY present
Step 3: If count = 4 or less, assign Score 9 or lower
Step 4: Do NOT assume fields. If not explicitly stated, field = missing
COMMON_MISTAKES: Do NOT assume email if not stated
Do NOT assume role if not confirmed

GPT_MODEL_SPECIFIC_INSTRUCTIONS.you_are_using_gpt: These instructions prevent common GPT scoring mistakes

common_mistake_1.mistake: Scoring voicemail screening as 5 instead of 0
common_mistake_1.fix: If 'stay on the line' or 'I'll see if available' and NO live prospect speaks = 0

common_mistake_2.fix: If transcript ends during agent's incomplete sentence = 1

common_mistake_3.fix: If 'I'll transfer you' but no one picks up = 4

common_mistake_4.fix: 'Tomorrow afternoon' without specific time + no email = 5 (not 9)

common_mistake_5.fix: If email contains profanity or responses are gibberish = 1

common_mistake_6.fix: Name only (no contact) = 5. Score 6 requires name AND contact.

verification_before_scoring: 1. Is there a live human prospect? (If NO → 0)
2. Did they hang up mid-sentence? (If YES → 1)
4. Is callback vague without contact? (If YES → likely 5)
5. For Score 9: Is time SPECIFIC + email captured?
6. For Score 10: Are ALL 5 fields present?

anti_inflation_rules: Near-term callback (2-7 days) without specific time = 5
When in doubt, choose lower score
CRITICAL: 'Stay on the line' with no pickup = 0 (NOT 5)
CRITICAL: 'I'll see if available' with no prospect = 0 (NOT 5)
CRITICAL: Score 10 requires ALL 5 fields explicitly present
If transcript ends during 'stay on line' or 'please hold' = likely 0
If gatekeeper says 'I don't have a direct line' = likely 3
'We're all set' + current state = 2 (rejection, not interest)
CRITICAL: Failed transfer WITH name = 5, WITHOUT name = 4
final_instruction: After analyzing the transcript silently using ALL the rules above, return exactly one integer from 0 to 10. Do not include any explanation, notes, or formatting. Return only the number.
